name: Military Home Loans
description_short: VA-approved mortgage lender specializing in home purchase and refinance loans for veterans and active duty service members since 2004.
description_long:
Military Home Loans is a VA-approved mortgage lender that has been serving veterans and active duty military members since 2004. The company explicitly states it is not affiliated with the Department of Veterans Affairs or any government agency, but operates as an approved lender within the VA loan program. Based on their website messaging and customer testimonials, they have facilitated over 2,000 transactions for military borrowers.

The company offers three primary services: new home purchase loans, refinancing, and a signature "Welcome Home Program" that covers up to $10,000 in closing costs. They advertise rapid pre-approval (24 hours), a 28-day closing promise with a $500 guarantee if delayed, and a $1,500 guarantee if their pre-approval is later denied. Their team includes named loan officers (Clay Murray, Shiraney Sim, Eric Edwards) and they emphasize transparent pricing with no hidden fees.

Military Home Loans differentiates itself through military-specific service offerings and performance guarantees uncommon in the mortgage industry. Their closing cost coverage program is prominently featured, and they highlight fast execution and responsive communication. The company maintains VA-specific educational resources including a VA Q&A section, VA Calendar, and VA Resources page to help borrowers understand their benefits.

The company appears legitimate based on website professionalism, named staff, specific performance guarantees, and consistent customer testimonials across multiple transactions. However, borrowers should verify their licensing, rates compared to other VA lenders, and the specific terms and conditions behind the promotional guarantees (*† symbols indicate terms apply). The $10,000 closing cost coverage is conditional on loan amount and program eligibility.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Up to $10,000 closing cost coverage through Welcome Home Program (eliminates major barrier to homeownership)
$1,500 pre-approval guarantee—if approval is later denied, company pays borrower
$500 close-on-time guarantee—pays borrower if closing exceeds 28 days
Fast pre-approval in as little as 24 hours
Named loan officers (Clay Murray, Shiraney Sim, Eric Edwards) with repeated customer relationships
Multiple customer testimonials citing third transactions, indicating customer retention and satisfaction
Comprehensive VA resources (Q&A, calendar, news) beyond typical mortgage offerings
Explicit transparency promise: "no hidden fees or surprises"
cons: All promotional guarantees have conditional terms marked with asterisks/daggers, requiring detailed disclosure review
Limited transparency on actual interest rates, APR, or how they compare to competing VA lenders
$10,000 closing cost coverage is dependent on loan amount (not specified what minimum qualifies)
No information provided about loan limits, debt-to-income requirements, or credit score minimums
Marketing claims (e.g., "flawless" refinances) lack objective verification beyond customer testimonials
best_for: Veterans and active duty service members purchasing a primary residence for the first time
Existing homeowners seeking VA loan refinancing with demonstrated military service
Military borrowers concerned about closing costs and seeking upfront cost reduction
Service members wanting dedicated military-focused lender familiar with VA benefit nuances
diagnosis: Military Home Loans is best suited for veterans and active duty members seeking a dedicated VA lender willing to cover substantial closing costs. The primary caveat is that all promotional guarantees carry conditional terms requiring verification, and borrowers should compare their rates and fees against other VA lenders and traditional banks before committing, despite the attractive upfront cost-coverage incentive.
services: Welcome Home Program (up to $10,000 closing cost coverage)
VA loan education and Q&A resources
Complete loan process guidance from application through closing
Expert guidance on VA benefits utilization
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 8885 Rio San Diego Dr Suite 347, San Diego, CA 92108
